source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A long wave packet with amplitude $A$ composed predominantly of frequencies very near $\omega_{0}$ propagates on an infinitely long string of linear mass density $\mu$ stretched with a tension $T$ as shown in Fig. 1.218. The packet encounters a bead of mass $m$ attached to the string as shown in the sketch. What is the amplitude of the transmitted wave packet?

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A long wave packet with amplitude $A$ composed predominantly of frequencies very near $\omega_{0}$ propagates on an infinitely long string of linear mass density $\mu$ stretched with a tension $T$ as shown in Fig. 1.218. The packet encounters a bead of mass $m$ attached to the string as shown in the sketch. What is the amplitude of the transmitted wave packet?

metadata.reasoning_chain: The equation of motion for the string for small transverse oscillations is a wave equation $\frac{\partial^{2} y}{\partial x^{2}}-\frac{\mu}{T} \frac{\partial^{2} y}{\partial t^{2}}=0$, with velocity $v=\pm\sqrt{T/\mu}$. For waves of angular frequency $\omega$, define $k=\frac{\omega}{v}=\omega\sqrt{\frac{\mu}{T}}$. For frequencies near $\omega_{0}$, the solutions are $y_{1}=A e^{i(kx-\omega_{0}t)}+B e^{-i(kx-\omega_{0}t)}$ for $x<0$ and $y_{2}=C e^{i(kx-\omega_{0}t)}$ for $x>0$. The continuity condition at $x=0$ gives $A+B=C$. The bead's motion is governed by $-m\omega_{0}^{2}C=-ikT(A-B)+ikTC$, leading to $A-B=\left(1+\frac{m\omega_{0}^{2}}{ikT}\right)C$. Combining with $A+B=C$ yields $C=\frac{2A}{\left(2+\frac{m\omega_{0}^{2}}{ikT}\right)}$, and hence the amplitude of the transmitted wave is $|C|=\frac{2A}{\sqrt{4+\frac{m^{2}\omega_{Q}^{4}}{k^{2}T^{2}}}}=\frac{2A}{\sqrt{4+\frac{m^{2}\omega_{0}^{\alpha}}{\mu T}}}$.

For waves of angular frequency $\omega$, define $k=\frac{\omega}{v}=\omega\sqrt{\frac{\mu}{T}}$. For frequencies near $\omega_{0}$, the solutions are $y_{1}=A e^{i(kx-\omega_{0}t)}+B e^{-i(kx-\omega_{0}t)}$ for $x<0$ and $y_{2}=C e^{i(kx-\omega_{0}t)}$ for $x>0$. The continuity condition at $x=0$ gives $A+B=C$. The bead's motion is governed by $-m\omega_{0}^{2}C=-ikT(A-B)+ikTC$, leading to $A-B=\left(1+\frac{m\omega_{0}^{2}}{ikT}\right)C$. Combining with $A+B=C$ yields $C=\frac{2A}{\left(2+\frac{m\omega_{0}^{2}}{ikT}\right)}$, and hence the amplitude of the transmitted wave is $|C|=\frac{2A}{\sqrt{4+\frac{m^{2}\omega_{Q}^{4}}{k^{2}T^{2}}}}=\frac{2A}{\sqrt{4+\frac{m^{2}\omega_{0}^{\alpha}}{\mu T}}}$.
